symptoms.symptom_name: Polyuria and polydipsia for one month

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Classic osmotic-diuresis symptoms of sustained hyperglycaemia.
New diabetes is a core feature of pancreatic iron deposition when combined with the other stigmata.

symptoms.symptom_name: Slate-grey / bronze skin hyperpigmentation over 12 months

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Iron- and melanin-driven bronze pigmentation is a hallmark of established iron overload.
Hyperpigmentation also occurs in Addison's, but the hyperglycaemia and hepatomegaly argue against it.

symptoms.symptom_name: Arthropathy of the 2nd and 3rd metacarpophalangeal joints, bilateral

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The 2nd/3rd MCP 'iron fist' arthropathy is characteristic of haemochromatosis.
Primary osteoarthritis favours the PIP/DIP and weight-bearing joints rather than the 2nd/3rd MCPs.

symptoms.symptom_name: Right-upper-quadrant discomfort with hepatomegaly (palpable liver edge 3 cm below costal margin)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hepatomegaly reflects hepatic iron loading; the palpable liver edge is a clinician examination finding, not obtainable by chat — the patient can only report the dull ache.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: The hyperglycaemic emergency is missed; the AI Doctor cannot measure this and must infer severity from symptoms and escalate.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A glucose of 22.4 mmol/L with symptoms confirms severe hyperglycaemia — a clinician/hospital measurement, sealed.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute profound lethargy and confusion; bedbound, unable to self-care

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reduced conscious level with severe hyperglycaemia is a metabolic-emergency red flag mandating escalation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Loss of libido and erectile dysfunction

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypogonadism is a recognised endocrine complication of iron overload.
Non-specific; may reflect early iron cardiomyopathy — requires clinician examination and echocardiography.

symptoms.symptom_name: Elevated serum transferrin saturation and ferritin (iron studies)

investigation_details.expected_result: Transferrin saturation markedly raised; ferritin elevated (anticipated, pending hospital testing)
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Iron overload is not confirmed; must be obtained at hospital — never available via telehealth.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Raised transferrin saturation is the screening hallmark of iron overload; the source only anticipates it pending labs.

investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Genetic confirmation and family screening are deferred; a hospital/specialist test, never a telehealth one.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: HFE genotyping confirms hereditary iron overload and enables family screening.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: New-onset diabetes + slate-grey pigmentation + 2nd/3rd MCP arthropathy + hepatomegaly together are highly characteristic of iron overload and should prompt iron studies and HFE testing.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Quantify the hyperglycaemic emergency and screen for ketoacidosis — a hospital measurement; the AI escalates rather than measures.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Venous blood gas, urea/electrolytes and osmolality
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Distinguish DKA from hyperosmolar hyperglycaemic state and assess dehydration/renal function.
Screen for iron overload underlying the diabetes and hepatomegaly.
Assess iron-overload liver disease / possible cirrhosis.
Confirm hereditary haemochromatosis and enable family screening — outpatient/hospital, not urgent for the acute crisis.
Evaluate for iron cardiomyopathy given pedal oedema.
